QF.id: XVI Clearing, Settlement and Risk Management_28

question.text: In peak margin reporting, exchanges calculate margins by taking how many trade snapshots per day?

answer.text: Four snapshots during the trading session
Two snapshots — at open and close

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under peak margin rules (2020-21), exchanges take four trade snapshots during the session to calculate peak exposure. This replaced the earlier end-of-day position-based system. **The Trap:** Not just end-of-day (that was the old system). Not two snapshots. Not continuous (though monitoring is ongoing). **Cross-Exam Context:** Peak margin rules apply across equity and commodity derivatives. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Peak margin means intraday leverage is restricted — brokers must collect full margins upfront, preventing the old practice of allowing excess intraday exposure with only EOD reporting.
